Farn PoLrrrcAL PnacrrcEs covrvrrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620. Sacramento, CA g5gl4_Z3}g
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322_0886
May 15,2003
Doreen Liberto-Blanck, AICp
370 Weymouth Street
Cambria, CA93428
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. 4-03-060
Dear Ms. Liberto-Blanck:
This letter is.il re_sqonse to your request for advice regarding the honoraria
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the..Act,).1
QUESTTON
May you write and publish a book regarding dispute resolution and land use
process, and co-author a book with your husband r"gurd^ing geologic impacts on land use
without violating the honorariaprovisions of the Act?
CONCLUSION
Yes, because books are expressly excluded from the definition of ..articles,,,
whose publication for remuneration is forbidden under the Act.
FACTS
As a planning commissioner for the County of San Luis obispo, you are subject
to the state's conflict-of-interest laws. You have been a practicing land'use and
environmental planner for more than twenty years and teach part-Iime in the Cal poly
university, San Luis obispo city and Regilnal Planning Department. you are a recent
graduate of Pepperdine School of Law wiih a master's algree in dispute resolution.
You want tg wri-te a book regarding dispute resolution and land use process , and a
book with your husband regarding giolog[ impacts on land use. you were advised that
' Government code sections 81000 - glo14. commission regulations appearat Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. 4-03-060
Page No. 2
there may be an exemption from the honorarium provision of the conflict of interest law
since you are writing about your profession, however, a written request should be made.
ANALYSIS
Government Code $ S9502(a)2 provides that no individual specified in section
87200, including planning commissioners, shall accept any honorarium. The term
"honorarium" is defined as "any payment made in consideration for any speech glven,
article published, or attendance at any public or private conference, "orrr"rtion, meeting,
social event, meal, or like gathering." (Section g9501(a).) Regulation lg93l.2 states:
"(a) Forpurposes of Government code sections 9950r through
89506, an 'article published' means a nonfictional written *ork,
"(1) That is produced in connection with any actiLityother than
the practice of a bona fide business, trade, or profession; and
"Q) That is published in aperiodical, journal, newspaper,
newsletter, magazine, pamphlet, or similar publication.
"(b) For puq)oses of this regulation, an individual receives
payment for an article published if he or she receives a payment
for:
"(1) Drafting any part of an article published, except activities
solely involving secretarial assistance.
"(2) Berngidentified as an author of or contributor to the article.
"(c) 'Article' does not include books, plays, or screenplays.,,
Because you are planning to write books, and books are excluded from the
definition of "article" under regulation 18931.2(c), you are not precluded from accepting
compensation for any books that you may write, either with your husband or alone.3'
2 All further statutory references are to the Govemment Code unless otherwise specified.
3 Of course, receipt of io"otrr" may result in the need for disclosure of future conflicts of interest.
we have enclosed ourpamphlet,"canl vote," for your information on this subject.


File No. A-03-060
Page No. 3
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
iltt(/
By: Holly B. Armstrong
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure
